solar_cf.label: Solar capacity factor
solar_cf.help: Mean solar PV output as a fraction of nameplate.

wind_cf.label: Wind capacity factor
wind_cf.help: Mean wind output as a fraction of nameplate.

mix_alpha.label: Optimal solar share α*
mix_alpha.help: Storage-optimal fraction of the build that is solar (rest is wind).

mix_s_tot_pct.label: Optimal-mix storage
mix_s_tot_pct.help: Storage to ride out the worst year, as % of annual consumption.

solar_s_tot_pct.label: Solar-only storage
solar_s_tot_pct.help: Storage for a solar-only build, % of annual consumption.

wind_s_tot_pct.label: Wind-only storage
wind_s_tot_pct.help: Storage for a wind-only build, % of annual consumption.

mix_f_adj.label: Overbuild factor
mix_f_adj.help: Excess installation (worst-year reliability) for the optimal mix.

assumptions.Model: Each region is sized to be self-sufficient on solar+wind+storage alone, meeting demand every year including the worst. The optimal mix is the solar share α* that minimizes storage.
assumptions.Overbuild (f_adj): Capacity is over-installed by the worst-year reliability factor f_adj so the weakest year still meets demand with storage.
assumptions.Storage: Round-trip + self-discharge: charge 0.9, discharge 0.8, annual energy retention 0.7.
assumptions.Land density: Solar 30 MW/km² (array footprint); wind 100 MW/km² (direct footprint — turbine pads + roads only; the ~98% spacing stays farmable).
assumptions.Land caps: Feasibility is tested at 1% of state land (NREL's <1% claim), 1.88% (2× US onshore oil & gas surface footprint), and 5% (all onshore oil & gas production land).
assumptions.Co-location (k): Overlap k lets the same footprint host (1+k)× generation (turbines + PV on one parcel), so land needed shrinks and more regions fit a cap as k rises (k = 0/17/35/50%).
assumptions.GLDAS vs NLDAS: GLDAS: global 3-hourly theoretical forcing with flat (normalized) demand. NLDAS-USA: hourly forcing over the CONUS with real monthly EIA consumption, so USA results are in real energy (TWh).
assumptions.Framing: This quantifies LOCAL self-sufficiency (per-state autarky), not national grid optimization — where our land/storage exceeds NREL's <1%, that gap is the externalized cost of the national transmission build-out.
